payload.prompt_preview: You are running inside a benchmark workspace. Solve the task using only the provided tools and files inside the workspace. Do not assume access to evaluation files. Return a concise final summary.
You are running inside a benchmark workspace. Solve the task using only the provided tools and files inside the workspace. Do not assume access to evaluation files. Return a concise final summary.

payload.output_preview:
Short summary (as of 15 March 2026)

1) Most recent enforcement milestone
- The most recent binding (legal) milestones are the staged application dates in the AI Act itself: Chapters I–II (including the prohibition list) applied from 2 February 2025; Chapter III §4, Chapter V (the rules for general‑purpose AI models), governance and penalties became applicable from 2 August 2025. The main remaining “full application” date for the Act is 2 August 2026 (most substantive high‑risk obligations). (Primary source: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689, Article 113). ([eur-lex.europa.eu](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1689/oj/eng))

- Important recent policy developments (that affect rollout/timing but are not law): the European Commission published a “Digital Omnibus” legislative proposal on 19 November 2025 (to amend parts of the AI Act among other digital rules), and the EDPB & EDPS issued a joint opinion on that proposal in January–February 2026.
